meta.name: BRS Brief Resilience Scale
meta.description: The BRS is a 6-item scale assessing the ability to bounce back or recover from stress. Resilience is the capacity to recover from difficult experiences. The scale comprises 3 positively worded and 3 negatively worded items measuring core resilience in coping with adversity.
meta.author: Smith et al. (2008)
meta.reference: Smith BW, Dalen J, Wiggins K, Steger MF, et al. The brief resilience scale: assessing the ability to bounce back. Int J Behav Med. 2008;15(3):194-200.
meta.instruction: Please indicate the extent to which you agree with each of the following statements.

questions.text: I tend to bounce back quickly after hard times
I have a hard time making it through stressful events
It does not take me long to recover from a stressful event
It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens
I usually come through difficult times with little trouble
I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience level is low. You may have difficulty recovering from stress and adversity. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Learn and practice stress management techniques
Build a social support network and seek help from others
Strengthen resilience through regular exercise, meditation, and other practices
If you consistently struggle to cope with difficulties, consider professional counseling
Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness

ranges.label: Below Average Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience is below average. You may need more time to recover from adversity in some situations. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Develop positive coping strategies
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits
Practice acceptance and mindfulness to reduce overreaction to difficulties
Find activities and ways to recharge
Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle

ranges.label: Normal Resilience
ranges.description: You have a normal level of resilience and can recover well from most stressors and adversity. Your well-being is at a moderate level. Increasing positive experiences and social connections may help improve your overall sense of fulfillment.
suggestions: Continue maintaining and strengthening your resilience resources
Flexibly apply your existing coping strategies when facing new challenges
Maintain positive social relationships and a healthy lifestyle
Increase the frequency of positive social activities
Develop a hobby that gives you a sense of accomplishment

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: You have high resilience and can recover quickly and effectively from stress and adversity. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your strong resilience
Your experience can help and encourage others
While helping others, remember to also look after your own mental health
Try doing one fulfilling activity each day
Stay connected with friends and family to build a sense of belonging
